<lean>
axiom Show : Type
axiom StrangerThings : Show
axiom BlackMirror : Show

axiom NetflixShow : Show → Prop
axiom Popular : Show → Prop
axiom Binge : Show → Prop        -- Karen binge-watches the show
axiom Download : Show → Prop     -- Karen downloads the show
axiom ShareLisa : Show → Prop    -- Karen shares the show with Lisa

-- "Stranger Things" is a popular Netflix show.
axiom ST_netflix : NetflixShow StrangerThings
axiom ST_popular : Popular StrangerThings

-- If a Netflix show is popular, Karen will binge-watch it.
axiom popular_implies_binge : ∀ s : Show, NetflixShow s → Popular s → Binge s

-- If and only if Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will download it.
axiom binge_iff_download_on_netflix : ∀ s : Show, NetflixShow s → (Binge s ↔ Download s)

-- Karen does not download "Black Mirror."
axiom not_download_BM : ¬ Download BlackMirror

-- "Black Mirror" is a Netflix show.
axiom BM_netflix : NetflixShow BlackMirror

-- If Karen binge-watches a Netflix show, she will share it with Lisa.
axiom binge_implies_share : ∀ s : Show, NetflixShow s → Binge s → ShareLisa s

-- From the biconditional on Netflix shows and not_download_BM, we can derive ¬ Binge BlackMirror.
theorem not_binge_BM : ¬ Binge BlackMirror :=
by
  have hiff : Binge BlackMirror ↔ Download BlackMirror :=
    binge_iff_download_on_netflix BlackMirror BM_netflix
  have hb_to_hd : Binge BlackMirror → Download BlackMirror := Iff.mp hiff
  intro hb
  exact not_download_BM (hb_to_hd hb)

-- Cannot prove Popular BlackMirror or its negation from the given axioms.
</lean>

ANSWER: Uncertain

axiom HighlyIntelligent : obj → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Alien x → Extraterrestrial x
-- If someone is from Mars, then they are an alien.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, FromMars x → Alien x
-- No extraterrestrials are human.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, Extraterrestrial x → ¬ Human x
-- All highly intelligent beings from Earth are humans.

-- If Marvin is not from Earth, then Marvin is an extraterrestrial.

  intro h
  have he : FromEarth Marvin := And.left h
  have hm : FromMars Marvin := And.right h
  have am : Alien Marvin := A2 Marvin hm

  have nhm : ¬ Human Marvin := A3 Marvin em
  have him : HighlyIntelligent Marvin := A5
  have hum : Human Marvin := A4 Marvin (And.intro him he)
  exact nhm hum

theorem not_earth_and_not_mars : ¬ FromEarth Marvin ∧ ¬ FromMars Marvin := by
  cases A6 with
  | inl h =>
      exact False.elim (not_earth_and_mars h)
  | inr h =>
      exact h

theorem not_from_mars : ¬ FromMars Marvin := And.right not_earth_and_not_mars

  fun h =>

    let d := h.right
    let es := A2 Taylor g
    let vc := A1 Taylor es
    let nvc := A4 Taylor d
    nvc vc

  Or.elim A6
    (fun hL => False.elim (not_left hL))
    (fun hR => hR)

theorem not_grew : ¬ GrewHCCH Taylor := right_branch.left
theorem not_disregard : ¬ DisregardWellBeing Taylor := right_branch.right

-- Predicates
axiom MultipleBirth : obj → Prop
axiom SpendTimeWithSiblings : obj → Prop

axiom ComplainsOften : obj → Prop
axiom LiveAtHome : obj → Prop
axiom LiveWithStrangers : obj → Prop
axiom Baby : obj → Prop

-- Rules from the context
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, MultipleBirth x → SpendTimeWithSiblings x
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, HaveSiblingsBornTogether x → MultipleBirth x
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, ComplainsOften x → HaveSiblingsBornTogether x
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, LiveAtHome x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, SpendTimeWithSiblings x → LiveAtHome x

-- Luke's disjunction:
-- Either (Baby and MultipleBirth and LiveWithStrangers) or (not (Baby and MultipleBirth) and not LiveWithStrangers)
axiom Luke_disj :
  (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) ∨
  (¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke) ∧ ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke)

-- Derived: MultipleBirth implies not LiveWithStrangers (by chaining R1, R5, R4)
theorem multipleBirth_no_strangers : ∀ x : obj, MultipleBirth x → ¬ LiveWithStrangers x :=
  fun x h => R4 x (R5 x (R1 x h))

-- Using the above, the first disjunct in Luke_disj is impossible, so we obtain the second disjunct.
theorem luke_not_baby_and_multi_and_not_strangers :
  (¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke)) ∧ (¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke) :=
by
  have not_first :
    ¬ (Baby Luke ∧ MultipleBirth Luke ∧ LiveWithStrangers Luke) :=
  by
    intro h
    have hMB : MultipleBirth Luke := h.right.left
    have hStr : LiveWithStrangers Luke := h.right.right
    have hNotStr : ¬ LiveWithStrangers Luke := multipleBirth_no_strangers Luke hMB
    exact hNotStr hStr

axiom LGA : Airport

  intro hfrom
  have hnot : ¬ (FliesFrom Susan LGA ∧ FliesTo Susan LGA) := R1 Susan LGA
  exact hnot (And.intro hfrom T1)

axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Spills x → ¬ Tidy x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, ClumsyFoodieWhoGoesOutFrequently x → Spills x

  intro x

  have hSpill : Spills x := And.left h

  have hNotTidy : ¬ Tidy x := A1 x hSpill
  have hTidy : Tidy x := A3 x hClean
  exact hNotTidy hTidy

axiom obj : Type
axiom Peter : obj

axiom Spill : obj → Prop
axiom Tidy : obj → Prop
axiom Cleanly : obj → Prop
axiom ValueOrderSpot : obj → Prop
axiom FamilyPrior : obj → Prop
axiom ClumsyFoodieGoingOut : obj → Prop

-- If people spill a lot of food on their clothing, then they are not notably tidy.
axiom R1 : ∀ x : obj, Spill x → ¬ Tidy x
-- All clumsy foodies who go out frequently to find new food restaurants to try spill a lot of food on their clothing.
axiom R2 : ∀ x : obj, ClumsyFoodieGoingOut x → Spill x
-- If people are cleanly, then they are notably tidy.
axiom R3 : ∀ x : obj, Cleanly x → Tidy x
-- If people value order and spotlessness, then they are cleanly.
axiom R4 : ∀ x : obj, ValueOrderSpot x → Cleanly x
-- All people whose families prioritize order and spotlessness themselves value order and spotlessness.
axiom R5 : ∀ x : obj, FamilyPrior x → ValueOrderSpot x
-- Peter is either (spills and cleanly) or (not spills and not cleanly)
axiom P1 : (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬Spill Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter)

-- We prove the negation of the queried statement:
-- ¬ (Peter is a clumsy foodie going out ∨ Peter's family prioritizes order and spotlessness)
theorem not_goal : ¬ (ClumsyFoodieGoingOut Peter ∨ FamilyPrior Peter) := by
  -- First show that Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter leads to a contradiction
  have hnot : ¬ (Spill Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) := by
    intro h
    have hs : Spill Peter := h.left
    have hc : Cleanly Peter := h.right
    have ht : Tidy Peter := R3 Peter hc
    have hnt : ¬ Tidy Peter := R1 Peter hs
    exact hnt ht
  -- Therefore, from P1, only the right disjunct holds: ¬Spill Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter
  have hrc : ¬Spill Peter ∧ ¬Cleanly Peter := Or.resolve_left P1 hnot
  have hns : ¬Spill Peter := hrc.left
  have hnc : ¬Cleanly Peter := hrc.right
  -- From ¬Spill Peter and (ClumsyFoodieGoingOut → Spill), derive ¬ClumsyFoodieGoingOut Peter
  have hnot_cf : ¬ ClumsyFoodieGoingOut Peter := by
    intro hcf
    have hs : Spill Peter := R2 Peter hcf
    exact hns hs
  -- From ¬Cleanly Peter and (FamilyPrior → ValueOrderSpot → Cleanly), derive ¬FamilyPrior Peter
  have hnot_fam : ¬ FamilyPrior Peter := by
    intro hf
    have hv : ValueOrderSpot Peter := R5 Peter hf
    have hc2 : Cleanly Peter := R4 Peter hv
    exact hnc hc2
  -- Conclude ¬ (ClumsyFoodieGoingOut Peter ∨ FamilyPrior Peter)
  intro hdisj
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hcf => exact hnot_cf hcf
  | inr hf => exact hnot_fam hf
</lean>

ANSWER: False

axiom Tidy : Person → Prop
axiom Cleanly : Person → Prop
axiom ValuesOrder : Person → Prop
axiom FamilyPrioritizes : Person → Prop
axiom ClumsyFoodieOutFrequently : Person → Prop

axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, Spills x → ¬ Tidy x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : Person, ClumsyFoodieOutFrequently x → Spills x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : Person, Cleanly x → Tidy x
axiom A4 : ∀ x : Person, ValuesOrder x → Cleanly x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : Person, FamilyPrioritizes x → ValuesOrder x
axiom A6 : (Spills Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) ∨ (¬ Spills Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter)

theorem peter_statement :
  (ClumsyFoodieOutFrequently Peter ∧ FamilyPrioritizes Peter) ∨
  (¬ ClumsyFoodieOutFrequently Peter ∧ ¬ FamilyPrioritizes Peter) := by
  -- Show (Spills ∧ Cleanly) is impossible
  have hContr : (Spills Peter ∧ Cleanly Peter) → False := by
    intro hsc
    have hs : Spills Peter := hsc.left
    have hc : Cleanly Peter := hsc.right
    have ht : Tidy Peter := A3 Peter hc
    have hnt : ¬ Tidy Peter := A1 Peter hs
    exact hnt ht
  -- Therefore we must have ¬Spills ∧ ¬Cleanly
  have hNotSNotC : ¬ Spills Peter ∧ ¬ Cleanly Peter := by
    apply Or.elim A6
    · intro hsc
      exact False.elim (hContr hsc)
    · intro hneg
      exact hneg
  have hNotS : ¬ Spills Peter := hNotSNotC.left
  have hNotC : ¬ Cleanly Peter := hNotSNotC.right
  -- From CF → Spills and ¬Spills, get ¬CF
  have hNotClumsy : ¬ ClumsyFoodieOutFrequently Peter := by
    intro hcf
    have hs : Spills Peter := A2 Peter hcf
    exact hNotS hs
  -- From FamilyPrioritizes → ValuesOrder → Cleanly and ¬Cleanly, get ¬FamilyPrioritizes
  have fp_implies_clean : ∀ x : Person, FamilyPrioritizes x → Cleanly x := by
    intro x hfp
    have hvo : ValuesOrder x := A5 x hfp
    exact A4 x hvo
  have hNotFP : ¬ FamilyPrioritizes Peter := by
    intro hfp
    have hc : Cleanly Peter := fp_implies_clean Peter hfp
    exact hNotC hc
  exact Or.inr ⟨hNotClumsy, hNotFP⟩
</lean>

<lean>
axiom obj : Type
axiom Tom : obj

axiom NiceToAnimals : obj → Prop
axiom MeanToAnimals : obj → Prop
axiom Grumpy : obj → Prop
axiom AnimalLover : obj → Prop
axiom PetOwner : obj → Prop

-- No one nice to animals is also mean to animals.
axiom A1 : ∀ x, NiceToAnimals x → ¬ MeanToAnimals x
-- Some grumpy people are mean to animals.
axiom A2 : ∃ x, Grumpy x ∧ MeanToAnimals x
-- All animal lovers are nice to animals.
axiom A3 : ∀ x, AnimalLover x → NiceToAnimals x
-- All pet owners love animals.
axiom A4 : ∀ x, PetOwner x → AnimalLover x
-- Tom is a pet owner.
axiom A5 : PetOwner Tom

-- From the axioms, Tom is an animal lover and thus not mean to animals.
theorem tom_animal_lover : AnimalLover Tom := A4 Tom A5
theorem tom_not_mean : ¬ MeanToAnimals Tom :=
  A1 Tom (A3 Tom tom_animal_lover)

-- The questioned statement:
-- If Tom is not both a grumpy person and mean to animals,
-- then Tom is neither a grumpy person nor an animal lover.
def Statement :=
  (¬ (Grumpy Tom ∧ MeanToAnimals Tom)) → (¬ Grumpy Tom ∧ ¬ AnimalLover Tom)

-- We show the statement is false by proving its negation.
theorem statement_false : ¬ Statement := by
  intro h
  -- antecedent is true because Tom is not mean to animals
  have hAnte : ¬ (Grumpy Tom ∧ MeanToAnimals Tom) := by
    intro hgm
    exact tom_not_mean hgm.right
  -- apply the implication to get the consequent
  have hCons : ¬ Grumpy Tom ∧ ¬ AnimalLover Tom := h hAnte
  -- but Tom is an animal lover, contradiction
  exact (And.right hCons) tom_animal_lover
</lean>

axiom Evocative : obj → Prop
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop

axiom Donated : obj → Prop

axiom T_or_loc : InNewMexico Picuris ∨ InTexas Picuris
axiom T_visit : Visited Juan Picuris

axiom T_loc_harding_picuris : LocatedIn HardingMine Picuris
axiom T_donated_harding : Donated HardingMine

-- There are no mountain ranges in Texas that have mines that have been donated.
axiom No_TX_MR_with_donated_mine :
  ∀ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ InTexas r → ¬ ∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m r ∧ Donated m

-- From the donated mine located in Picuris, deduce Picuris is not in Texas.
theorem picuris_not_in_texas : ¬ InTexas Picuris :=
by
  intro htx
  have hmr : MountainRange Picuris := T_mr_picuris
  have hno := No_TX_MR_with_donated_mine Picuris ⟨hmr, htx⟩
  have hex : ∃ m : obj, Mine m ∧ LocatedIn m Picuris ∧ Donated m :=
    ⟨HardingMine, And.intro T_mine_harding (And.intro T_loc_harding_picuris T_donated_harding)⟩
  exact hno hex

-- Optionally, deduce Picuris is in New Mexico (not needed for the main question).
theorem picuris_in_new_mexico : InNewMexico Picuris :=
by
  cases T_or_loc with
  | inl hnm => exact hnm
  | inr htx => exact False.elim (picuris_not_in_texas htx)

def Statement : Prop := ∃ r : obj, MountainRange r ∧ InTexas r ∧ Visited Juan r

-- We do know Juan visited Picuris, and Picuris is not in Texas:
theorem visited_picuris : Visited Juan Picuris := T_visit
theorem not_in_texas_picuris : ¬ InTexas Picuris := picuris_not_in_texas
-- But nothing rules out Juan possibly visiting some other Texas mountain range.
-- Hence Statement is not derivable, and neither is its negation.
</lean>

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom M : obj → Prop            -- Max adores
axiom Z : obj → Prop            -- Zaha Hadid's design style
axiom K : obj → Prop            -- Kelly Wearstler's design style
axiom B : obj → Prop            -- brutalist building
axiom I : obj → Prop            -- has interesting geometries
axiom E : obj → Prop            -- evocative
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop       -- dreamy

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Z x ∧ M x → I x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, B x ∧ M x → ¬ I x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, M x → (Z x ∨ K x)
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, K x ∧ M x → E x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, K x ∧ M x → Dreamy x
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, M x ∧ I x → B x ∧ E x

-- Theorem: Every design Max adores is either evocative or dreamy.
theorem max_design_evoc_or_dreamy : ∀ x : obj, M x → (E x ∨ Dreamy x) := by
  intro x hxM
  have hZorK : Z x ∨ K x := A3 x hxM
  -- Show that Max cannot adore any Zaha Hadid design style (leads to contradiction)
  have hNotZandM : ¬ (Z x ∧ M x) := by
    intro hZM
    have hI : I x := A1 x hZM
    have hB_and_E : B x ∧ E x := A6 x ⟨hxM, hI⟩
    have hB : B x := hB_and_E.left
    have hNotI : ¬ I x := A2 x ⟨hB, hxM⟩
    exact hNotI hI
  -- Therefore, from Z x ∨ K x and M x, we must have K x
  have hK : K x := by
    cases hZorK with
    | inl hZ => exact False.elim (hNotZandM ⟨hZ, hxM⟩)
    | inr hK => exact hK
  -- From K x and M x, we get both evocative and dreamy
  have hE : E x := A4 x ⟨hK, hxM⟩
  have hD : Dreamy x := A5 x ⟨hK, hxM⟩
  exact Or.inl hE
</lean>

axiom obj : Type

-- Predicates
axiom M : obj → Prop            -- Max adores
axiom Z : obj → Prop            -- Zaha Hadid's design style
axiom K : obj → Prop            -- Kelly Wearstler's design style
axiom B : obj → Prop            -- brutalist building
axiom I : obj → Prop            -- has interesting geometries
axiom E : obj → Prop            -- evocative
axiom Dreamy : obj → Prop       -- dreamy

-- Axioms from the context
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, Z x ∧ M x → I x
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, B x ∧ M x → ¬ I x
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, M x → (Z x ∨ K x)
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, K x ∧ M x → E x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, K x ∧ M x → Dreamy x
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, M x ∧ I x → B x ∧ E x

-- Theorem: Every design Max adores is either evocative or dreamy.
theorem max_design_evoc_or_dreamy : ∀ x : obj, M x → (E x ∨ Dreamy x) := by
  intro x hxM
  have hZorK : Z x ∨ K x := A3 x hxM
  -- Show that Max cannot adore any Zaha Hadid design style (leads to contradiction)
  have hNotZandM : ¬ (Z x ∧ M x) := by
    intro hZM
    have hI : I x := A1 x hZM
    have hB_and_E : B x ∧ E x := A6 x ⟨hxM, hI⟩
    have hB : B x := hB_and_E.left
    have hNotI : ¬ I x := A2 x ⟨hB, hxM⟩
    exact hNotI hI
  -- Therefore, from Z x ∨ K x and M x, we must have K x
  have hK : K x := by
    cases hZorK with
    | inl hZ => exact False.elim (hNotZandM ⟨hZ, hxM⟩)
    | inr hK => exact hK
  -- From K x and M x, we get both evocative and dreamy
  have hE : E x := A4 x ⟨hK, hxM⟩
  have hD : Dreamy x := A5 x ⟨hK, hxM⟩
  exact Or.inl hE

inductive Season where
| Spring : Season
| Summer : Season
| Fall : Season
| Winter : Season

inductive Person where
| Emma : Person
| Mia : Person
| James : Person

open Season Person

axiom Fav : Person → Season
axiom Student : Person → Prop
axiom WantsLongVacation : Person → Prop

-- All students who want to have a long vacation have summer as their favorite season.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : Person, Student x → WantsLongVacation x → Fav x = Summer

-- Emma's favorite season is summer.
axiom A2 : Fav Emma = Summer

-- Mia's favorite season is not the same as Emma's.
axiom A3 : Fav Mia ≠ Fav Emma

-- James wants to have a long vacation.
axiom A4 : WantsLongVacation James

-- From A2 and A3, we can deduce that Mia's favorite season is not summer.
theorem Mia_not_Summer : Fav Mia ≠ Summer := by
  intro h
  have h' : Fav Mia = Fav Emma := by
    exact Eq.trans h (Eq.symm A2)

axiom legs_imply_jump : ∀ x : obj, Animal x ∧ HasLegs x → Jumps x
-- If an animal is terricolous, then it has legs.

  exact h.right

  intro hd
  have h := dogs_bark x hd
  exact h.left

-- From the special condition, we can show KiKi cannot simultaneously lack jumping and legs.
theorem kiki_not_both_no_jump_and_no_legs : ¬ (¬ Jumps KiKi ∧ ¬ HasLegs KiKi) := by
  intro h
  have hdisj := kiki_special h
  cases hdisj with
  | inl hterr =>
      have hlegs : HasLegs KiKi := terricolous_implies_legs KiKi (And.intro kiki_animal hterr)
      exact h.right hlegs
  | inr hlegs =>
      exact h.right hlegs

-- We cannot derive Dog KiKi (and thus cannot derive Dog KiKi ∧ Barks KiKi) from the given axioms,

axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Monkey x → Mammal x

axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Bird x → CanFly x
axiom A5 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → Animal x → CanBreathe x
axiom A6 : ∀ x : obj, Pet x → CanFly x → HasWings x
axiom A7 : Pet Rock
axiom A8 : CanFly Rock ∨ Bird Rock ∨ ¬ CanBreathe Rock

theorem rock_has_wings : HasWings Rock := by
  have hpet : Pet Rock := A7
  cases A8 with
  | inl hfly =>
      exact A6 Rock hpet hfly
  | inr h2 =>
      cases h2 with
      | inl hbird =>
          exact A6 Rock hpet (A4 Rock hpet hbird)
      | inr hnotb =>
          have hMB : Monkey Rock ∨ Bird Rock := A3 Rock hpet
          have hB : Bird Rock := by
            cases hMB with

                have hMam : Mammal Rock := A2 Rock hM
                have hAn : Animal Rock := A1 Rock hpet hMam
                have hBr : CanBreathe Rock := A5 Rock hpet hAn
                exact False.elim (hnotb hBr)
            | inr hB' =>
                exact hB'
          exact A6 Rock hpet (A4 Rock hpet hB)

  exact bonnie_not_engaged_and_not_student ⟨hE, hns⟩

  exact hne hE

-- No baked sweets are spicy.
axiom A1 : ∀ x : obj, BakedSweet x → ¬ Spicy x
-- All cupcakes are baked sweets.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Cupcake x → BakedSweet x
-- All mala hotpots are spicy.
axiom A3 : ∀ x : obj, MalaHotpot x → Spicy x
-- All products from Baked by Melissa are cupcakes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, BBMProduct x → Cupcake x
-- Dried Thai chilies are spicy or mala hotpots or not baked sweets.
axiom A5 : Spicy DTC ∨ MalaHotpot DTC ∨ ¬ BakedSweet DTC

theorem dried_thai_chilies_not_bbm : ¬ BBMProduct DTC := by
  intro hBBM
  have hCup : Cupcake DTC := A4 DTC hBBM
  have hBaked : BakedSweet DTC := A2 DTC hCup
  cases A5 with
  | inl hSpicy =>
      have hNotSpicy : ¬ Spicy DTC := A1 DTC hBaked
      exact hNotSpicy hSpicy
  | inr h =>
      cases h with
      | inl hMala =>
          have hSp : Spicy DTC := A3 DTC hMala
          have hNotSp : ¬ Spicy DTC := A1 DTC hBaked
          exact hNotSp hSp
      | inr hNotBaked =>
          exact hNotBaked hBaked
</lean>

axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, Cupcake x → BakedSweet x
-- All mala hotpots are spicy.

-- All products from Baked by Melissa are cupcakes.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, FromBBM x → Cupcake x

theorem dried_thai_neither_bbm_nor_baked : ¬ FromBBM DriedThaiChilies ∧ ¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := by
  -- First, show Dried Thai chilies are not baked sweets.
  have not_baked : ¬ BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := by
    apply Or.elim A5
    · intro hS
      intro hBS
      have hNS : ¬ Spicy DriedThaiChilies := A1 DriedThaiChilies hBS
      exact hNS hS
    · intro h2
      apply Or.elim h2
      · intro hM
        intro hBS

        have hNS : ¬ Spicy DriedThaiChilies := A1 DriedThaiChilies hBS
        exact hNS hS
      · intro hNB
        exact hNB
  -- Then, use that to show they are not from Baked by Melissa.
  have not_bbm : ¬ FromBBM DriedThaiChilies := by
    intro hBBM
    have hCup : Cupcake DriedThaiChilies := A4 DriedThaiChilies hBBM
    have hBS : BakedSweet DriedThaiChilies := A2 DriedThaiChilies hCup
    exact not_baked hBS
  exact And.intro not_bbm not_baked

  (ProvideTO H ∧ ManyNeg H) → (RG4 H ∧ ¬ ProvideTO H) :=

  -- From A5 and A4, deduce RG4 H; then A2 gives Listed H; A1 gives ¬ ManyNeg H.
  have hRG4 : RG4 H := Or.elim A5 (fun h => h) (fun hp => A4 H hp)
  have hListed : Listed H := A2 H hRG4
  have hNotNeg : ¬ ManyNeg H := A1 H hListed
  intro h
  have : False := hNotNeg h.right
  exact False.elim this
</lean>

-- If a restaurant is listed in Yelp’s recommendations, then it has not received many negative reviews.

-- All restaurants with a rating greater than four are listed in Yelp’s recommendations.
axiom A2 : ∀ x : obj, RatingGT4 x → Listed x
-- Some restaurants that do not provide take-out service receive many negative reviews.

-- All restaurants that are popular among local residents have ratings greater than four.
axiom A4 : ∀ x : obj, PopularLocal x → RatingGT4 x
-- The Hamden Plaza Subway store has a rating greater than four, or it is popular among local residents.
axiom A5 : RatingGT4 HamdenPlazaSubway ∨ PopularLocal HamdenPlazaSubway

-- Goal: The Hamden Plaza Subway store provides take-out service or does not receive many negative reviews.
theorem hamden_takeout_or_notmanyneg :
  TakeOutService HamdenPlazaSubway ∨ ¬ ManyNegReviews HamdenPlazaSubway := by
  have hR : RatingGT4 HamdenPlazaSubway :=
    Or.elim A5 (fun h => h) (fun hpop => A4 HamdenPlazaSubway hpop)
  have hL : Listed HamdenPlazaSubway := A2 HamdenPlazaSubway hR
  have hNotNeg : ¬ ManyNegReviews HamdenPlazaSubway := A1 HamdenPlazaSubway hL
  exact Or.inr hNotNeg
</lean>

  apply Or.elim A5
  · intro hr

  · intro hp
    exact A4 Hamden hp

-- Therefore, Hamden is listed and hence does not have many negative reviews.

  have hL : Listed Hamden := A2 Hamden hamden_rating
  exact A1 Hamden hL

-- We cannot derive ¬TakeOut Hamden or TakeOut Hamden from the given axioms.
-- The existential A3 only asserts the existence of some restaurant with no take-out and many negatives,
-- which need not be Hamden, and we have ¬ManyNeg Hamden.

-- The Hamden Plaza Subway store has a rating > 4, or it is popular.

  apply Or.elim A5
  · intro hr

  · intro hp

  have hL : Listed Hamden := A2 Hamden hamden_rating
  exact A1 Hamden hL

-- Hence the statement "Hamden does not provide take-out service" cannot be proven or refuted.

axiom GoodFightsBad : ∀ x, GoodGuy x → ∃ y, BadGuy y ∧ Fights x y
axiom BadFightsGood : ∀ y, BadGuy y → ∃ x, GoodGuy x ∧ Fights y x
-- If somebody wins a fight, the person they are fighting does not win.
axiom WinFightOppNotWin : ∀ x y, Fights x y → Wins x → ¬ Wins y

  have gd : GoodGuy SirDigby := NamedAfterGood TheMovie SirDigby sm na
  have inm : InMovie SirDigby TheMovie := NamedAfterIn TheMovie SirDigby sm na
  have win_sd : Wins SirDigby := GoodGuysWin TheMovie SirDigby sm inm gd
  have f : Fights SirDigby Nemesis := SirDigby_fights_nemesis
  exact WinFightOppNotWin SirDigby Nemesis f win_sd

<lean>
axiom obj : Type

-- Entities
axiom Tom : obj
axiom Fluffy : obj
axiom OliveGarden : obj

-- Predicates and functions
axiom Managed : obj → Prop
axiom AllowsPets : obj → Prop
axiom Cat : obj → Prop
axiom Pet : obj → Prop
axiom AllowedToMoveInWith : obj → obj → obj → Prop  -- person, pet, building
axiom WillRent : obj → obj → Prop                   -- person, building
axiom MonthlyRent : obj → Nat
axiom SecurityDepositNoMoreThan : obj → Nat → Prop  -- building, dollar threshold

-- Facts
axiom some_managed_allow_pets : ∃ b : obj, Managed b ∧ AllowsPets b
axiom olive_is_managed : Managed OliveGarden
axiom fluffy_is_cat : Cat Fluffy
axiom cats_are_pets : ∀ x : obj, Cat x → Pet x
axiom rent_olive_is_2000 : MonthlyRent OliveGarden = 2000
axiom gt_1500_2000 : 1500 < 2000

-- Rules
-- If a managed building allows pets, then any person is allowed to move in with a pet.
axiom allow_rule :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → AllowsPets b →
  ∀ p a : obj, Pet a → AllowedToMoveInWith p a b

-- Deposit at a managed building is at least the monthly rent:
-- If the deposit is ≤ n, then the monthly rent is ≤ n.
axiom deposit_at_least_rent :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b → ∀ n : Nat,
  SecurityDepositNoMoreThan b n → MonthlyRent b ≤ n

-- Tom will rent in a managed building if allowed to move in with Fluffy and deposit ≤ $1500.
axiom tom_rent_rule :
  ∀ b : obj, Managed b →
  AllowedToMoveInWith Tom Fluffy b →
  SecurityDepositNoMoreThan b 1500 →
  WillRent Tom b

-- From rent(OliveGarden)=2000 and deposit≥rent, we can deduce deposit is not ≤ 1500 at OliveGarden.
lemma not_deposit_le_1500_olive : ¬ SecurityDepositNoMoreThan OliveGarden 1500 := by
  intro hdep
  have h_le : MonthlyRent OliveGarden ≤ 1500 :=
    deposit_at_least_rent OliveGarden olive_is_managed 1500 hdep
  have h2000_le_1500 : 2000 ≤ 1500 := by
    simpa [rent_olive_is_2000] using h_le
  exact (Nat.not_le.mpr gt_1500_2000) h2000_le_1500

-- Target statement would be:
-- theorem rent_olivegarden : WillRent Tom OliveGarden := ?
-- We cannot prove this, nor its negation, from the given information.
</lean>
